name: Fred Astaire
abstract: Fred Astaire (born Frederick Austerlitz, May 10, 1899 – June 22, 1987) was an American dancer, actor, singer, musician, choreographer, and presenter, whose career in stage, film, and television spanned 76 years. He is widely regarded as the "greatest popular-music dancer of all time".

works_created.t: If Swing Goes, I Go Too

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I was a Fred Astaire freak. So I’d walk two miles... and go see Fred Astaire in South Fork, Pennsylvania, just so I could try to learn those songs.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Middleburgh, New York, May 11, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: You ever hear that song? ... Fred Astaire made a recording of that with Benny Goodman’s band ... Great, great record
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: we have the dance medley with all songs about dancing. Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: wanting to dance like Fred Astaire and hold a pretty lady like Ginger Rogers as you did it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Lloyd
about_quotes.evidence: They had seen Fred Astaire and Cary Grant, so they were coming out of that too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Lloyd — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John W. Bubbles
about_quotes.evidence: I told him I had to see my manager because I didn't know what to charge or what to do, because I thought he was just a friend of mine so he saw my manager and my manager charged him $400 a lesson
